introduction: When multiple transactions execute concurrently, the data can become inconsistent. Concurrency control mechanisms like Locking and Timestamping ensure Isolation is maintained.

sections.title: Concurrency Problems
sections.content: Common issues include the Lost Update anomaly (one transaction overwrites another), Dirty Read (reading uncommitted data), and Non-repeatable Read (reading the same data twice and getting different results).

sections.title: Locks
sections.content: A Shared Lock (S-Lock) allows reading but not writing. An Exclusive Lock (X-Lock) allows both reading and writing. No other transaction can hold a lock if an X-Lock is held.

sections.title: Deadlocks
sections.content: A deadlock occurs when two or more transactions are waiting for locks held by each other, creating a circular wait. The DBMS resolves this by choosing a 'victim' transaction and aborting it.
